notes: Absorbed into Security Dynamics/RSA Security after July 1997 acquisition; technology elements survived in RSA portfolio
SSO became foundational enterprise security architecture; modern implementations via SAML OAuth OIDC (Okta Ping Identity Microsoft AD)
RSA algorithm remained industry standard for asymmetric encryption; RSA Data Security acquired by Security Dynamics 1996
X.509 became the universal standard for digital certificates; used in TLS/HTTPS today
Supported Windows 3.1 Windows 95 Windows NT desktop; transparent SSO and disk encryption
Pre-tested application connectors for Oracle Sybase Informix Unix networking; absorbed after acquisition
Central authorization server for Unix platforms (Digital HP-UX AIX Solaris); NT Server support in field test as of study
APIs and libraries for integrating custom applications with BoKS; used by DynaSoft for BoKS Connect module production
Software-based credential container independent of specific credential implementation; concept evolved into modern software tokens
Common Data Interchange Format; used by BoKS for metadata exchange with CASE tools and repositories; now deprecated
HP's SSO product incorporating DynaSoft BoKS technology; discontinued as HP security portfolio evolved
Sun's SSO/security management product using DynaSoft as strategic partner; discontinued after Oracle acquisition of Sun
